name: deadline@maggiehassan.com
subject: Got to meet this goal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd774/2a380d31/119f1a0a/fa19ce/3532663896/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd774/2a380d31/119f1a0a/fa19ce/3532663896/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
This could be bad --
The latest WMUR poll shows Governor Hassan's already small lead _shrinking_ in her race which will almost certainly decide Senate control. That's why we set a Rapid Response goal for $75,000 by midnight TONIGHT -- but right now our fundraising is about to fall behind too:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd774/2a380d31/119f1a0a/fa19cf/3532663896/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwOTA0X1dNVVJQb2xsRGVhZGxpbmVfdjIiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160904_WMURPollDeadline_v2&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
If we want to swing the polls, elect Maggie, and take back the Senate from the Trump-supporting, obstructionist Republicans, we've got to meet this goal and _every goal_ from now through Election Day. **[Please, will you give $3 or whatever you can to help Maggie reach this make-or-break $75,000 goal by midnight TONIGHT? When you do, you'll help get us back on track to winning in November and flipping the Senate blue.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd774/2a380d31/119f1a0a/fa19c8/3532663896/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwOTA0X1dNVVJQb2xsRGVhZGxpbmVfdjIiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160904_WMURPollDeadline_v2&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

**[Or, click here to donate another amount.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd774/2a380d31/119f1a0a/fa19c8/3532663896/VEsO/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwOTA0X1dNVVJQb2xsRGVhZGxpbmVfdjIiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160904_WMURPollDeadline_v2&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")**

name: Greg Gianforte
subject: Travis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greg for Montana, PO
body:
Greg for Montana

Share our new ad!

Friend,

I often tell a story about Travis
when I'm out on the campaign trail. This farm kid from Dillon,
getting sent out of state on a client visit at RightNow
Technologies. Our client couldn't believe it when Travis
completed the project, not only on time, but under budget.

Travis is an example that with
the Montana work ethic, the sky is the limit. Today, he is
growing high wage tech jobs in his home town of Dillon, Montana.

I'm in Dillon today for Labor
Day, celebrating the hard working men and women that put food on
the table and provide for our communities.

With just 64 days left before
Election Day, I want to make it clear - we're running to create
high wage jobs for Montana, stand up for our natural resource
industries, and restore accountability to the Governor's office.

Will you join us?

Could you share this message
from Travis, and help us keep this ad running? Just $20, $35 or
$50 - we're grateful for whatever you can give and to have you
on the team.

Thanks,
Greg

Paid for by Greg for Montana, PO
Box 1557, Helena, MT, 59624, Republican

name: John Gregg
subject: What Labor Day means to me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gregg for Indiana
body:
This is a time to celebrate the hard-working people who make our state great. It's a time to remember the people who organized to protect workers' rights, make our workplaces safer, and to give us the weekend.
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/3078680497283794944/-6237062671061612544?utm_campaign=engg_labday_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160905_engg_gq_labday_ful__jg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)
---
Peter, This weekend may be the unofficial end of summer -- and a great chance to spend time with friends and family. But Labor Day is more than just a day off. This is a time to celebrate the hard-working people who make our state great. It's a time to remember the people who organized to protect workers' rights, make our workplaces safer, and to give us the weekend. And just as important, today is a day to refresh and prepare for the fights ahead. Over the last four years, everyday Hoosiers have struggled. Too many are working too hard for too little, and it's time to put our state back on the right track. As your next governor, I'll work for Indiana, and I'll work for you. And today, my running mate Christina Hale and I are reaching out to voters at Labor Day parades in Boonville and Lowell to spread our message. But no matter where you are, and no matter how you celebrate today, I want to wish you a Happy Labor Day, from me, and our entire team. Happy Labor Day, John
| | [**Donate ->**](https://act.myngp.com/el/3078680497283794944/-6237062671061612544?utm_campaign=engg_labday_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160905_engg_gq_labday_ful__jg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)
---

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/3078680497283794944/-6092947482985756672) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/3078680497283794944/-6020889888947828736)
Paid for by Gregg for Indiana
---
Gregg for Indiana
PO Box 44224
Indianapolis IN 46244 United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/3078680497283794944/-6309120265099540480).

name: Katie McGinty
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1660139275200495616/6639175564441681920)
---
Friends --

Today, Labor Day, we proudly honor the American Labor movement and the working families who drive our nation forward. It's our duty to make sure they are not forgotten tomorrow.

70 years ago, our government made a deal with America's coal miners. The Krug-Lewis Agreement, signed by President Harry Truman, guaranteed that coal miners and their families would be secure in their retirement by providing a pension and health benefits.

But that promise is dissolving. Coal production and prices are down. Coal companies, one after another, have filed for bankruptcy. The impact of the 2008 financial crisis remains heavily felt, including by mining families here in Pennsylvania.

**If Congress doesn 't act now, the deal could soon be insolvent. The hardworking miners and their families -- who held up their end of the bargain -- could be left with nothing.**

[**Friends, that 's unacceptable and wrong. Join me in asking Congress to support the Miners Protection Act today to ensure that our government makes good on the promise we made all those years ago >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1660139275200495616/6639175564441681920)

For me, honoring miners is personal. My oldest brother was a coal miner out West until lung cancer tragically took his life, while another brother continues to work proudly in the mines in Wyoming. I know how hard miners work every day. I know how much their families worry for their safety and health.

Our nation owes every single coal miner a debt of gratitude. It is their hard, often dangerous, work that lit up America for decades, and now they deserve their due.

[**Tell Congress that our country 's miners deserve better than words of empty thanks. We need to hold up our end of the bargain >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1660139275200495616/6639175564441681920)

[**www.katiemcginty.com/miners**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1660139275200495616/6639175564441681920)

Thank you,

Katie McGinty

| [**DONATE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1660139275200495616/6783290752517537792?refcode=e20160905&refcode2=e20160905nd&amount=25)
---
---
| | Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1660139275200495616/6567117970403753984).

Katie McGinty for Senate
PO Box 22447
Philadelphia PA 19110 United States

name: Chris Koster
subject: The workers we fight for every day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/4022748668682962944/-2706240563203143680?utm_campaign=vv_lbrdy_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&ms=em16160905vvlbrdygqfulck&refcode=em16160905vvlbrdygqfulck)

Dear Friend,
Labor Day is about a lot more than picnics and parades. Today, we take time to celebrate the contributions workers have made to our country -- and recommit ourselves to fighting for an economy that works for every Missourian.

We have a choice before us now, just like generations before us: Do we build an economy designed to serve just special interests and a billionaire class? Or do we ensure that our neighbors and family members who work hard and play by the rules can put food on their tables, and give their children the futures they deserve?

That's what's at stake now, in Missouri and across America.

I've been thinking a lot lately about the Rulo Bridge, which connects Holt County in Northwest Missouri to Nebraska. As we celebrate workers and the contributions of the labor movement, [**I hope you 'll take a moment to watch this video, and then share with friends.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/4022748668682962944/-2706240563203143680?utm_campaign=vv_lbrdy_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&ms=em16160905vvlbrdygqfulck&refcode=em16160905vvlbrdygqfulck)

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/4022748668682962944/-2706240563203143680?utm_campaign=vv_lbrdy_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&ms=em16160905vvlbrdygqfulck&refcode=em16160905vvlbrdygqfulck)

As Governor, I will always stand up for a strong middle class and an economy that works for everyone. Thanks for standing with me in this fight for the future of Missouri's families.

Thank you,

Chris Koster

[DONATE](https://act.myngp.com/el/4022748668682962944/-2562125375127287808?utm_campaign=fnd_lbrdy_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_160905_fnd_gq_lbrdy_ful__ck)

****
****

Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer
---
Koster for Missouri
PO Box 1551
Jefferson City MO 65102 United States

name: Evan Bayh
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, PO Box 133 W
body:
| [ ](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50476/7be36b49/1ef40913/78f88b0c/213902578/VEsH/ "http://www.evanbayhforindiana.com/")
---
Friend, I know you all don't ask for thanks very often. But this Labor Day, I want to take a moment to say how grateful I am to all of you -- the hard working people of Indiana. Hoosiers are thoughtful, selfless and and work tirelessly to ensure a better future for Indiana families. I consider myself lucky to have grown up surrounded by these kinds of values. So thank you for all that you do this Labor Day. I hope I can make you as proud as you've made me.

Thank you for your support,

Evan ******[Contribute](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50476/7be36b49/1ef40913/78f88b13/213902578/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-august-eom2?refcode=20160905&amount=25")******
Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, PO Box 133 W. Market Street #153, Indianapolis, IN 46204

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: Labor Day thanks
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
|

Friend,

I'm very grateful for the overwhelming response to our end-of-August efforts. You stepped up to help us meet our goal and stay on track to keep our ground game going strong.

Thank you!

As we spend this Labor Day with friends and family, our thoughts go to those who have courageously fought to advance workers' rights in this country and around the world.

The fight for those rights is vitally important today. This Labor Day let's recommit to the goal of a 21st century social contract that matches the needs of our time, including equal pay for equal work, affordable high quality child care, and earned paid sick leave. And, in an age where education and training are more important than ever, let's build an education system that gives every hardworking student the opportunity to succeed in today's labor market.

Thanks for your commitment to the goals and priorities that American workers deserve.

Chris
---

Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
---
We believe that emails are a vital way to stay in direct contact with supporters. You're receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com. [**Click here**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a1c/70cdc2ba/47142e63/3974ad9/2843688361/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Click+here&utm_campaign=em090316_1&source=em090316_1") if you'd like to unsubscribe from these messages. Please enter dpwillis67@gmail.com to ensure that we are able to unsubscribe you from our list.

name: =?UTF-8?Q?Kellyanne=20Conway=2C=20Team=20Trump?=
subject: I want your honest feedback on our campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
I am Donald Trump’s campaign manager. |

Peter, I am proud to serve as Mr. Trump’s campaign manager. Yesterday I asked him to give me a list of our strongest supporters. Your name showed up on the list as a Big League Supporter. Which is why I’m asking for your valuable input on the state of the race and the issues you care about. Please complete the Platform for America Survey today. You’ve probably received a few surveys from us over the past month already. These surveys play a crucial role in helping us shape our message and ensuring we’re on track to WIN. But we still have a lot to do, and little time to do it. We have LESS than 70 days to: Broaden our support and win over the hearts of undecided voters Fight back against Hillary’s slanderous attacks with ads of our own Execute a massive, nationwide grassroots ground game Your honest, critical feedback helps us keep our campaign on message -- and ensures it’s resonating with the American people. Take the Survey Now >> I don’t have to tell you -- the mainstream media is always going to try to distort our message. But my job is to help cut through the media’s noise and get our message straight to the voter. To successfully do that, I need your help. I need to know where you stand on a variety of issues facing our country. Between now and November 8, every single day counts. Please make it your priority to complete the Platform for America Survey. Thank you, Peter. I look forward to reading your responses, Kellyanne Conway Campaign Manager Donald Trump for President TAKE SURVEY NOW

Please complete the Platform for America Survey today.
------------------------------------------------------

name: Jay Inslee
subject: A stronger economy. A better future.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
It's Labor Day -- and there's work ahead.

Dear Friend,

This Labor Day, we celebrate the working people whose grit and determination built our country -- and whose work continues to be the backbone of our economy. It's a time to reflect on the economy we're building here in Washington, and the work we have ahead of us.

When I took the oath of office in 2013, our Washington economy was fragile. We were emerging from the worst recession since the Great Depression.

Now, three years later, I'm proud of what we've been able to achieve together:

- Creating over 250,000 new jobs
- Achieving the lowest unemployment rate since 2008
- Making college more affordable for thousands of students
- Expanding export opportunities for Washington businesses

We have a lot to be proud of -- and a lot more to do.

Every day, I think about how we can build a thriving economy that creates good-paying jobs in every corner of our state, especially in our key industries -- agriculture, forestry, aerospace, military, life sciences, maritime, clean energy, and information technology.

We must keep building Washington's future together.

That's why I'm asking you for your help. We can't afford to change direction when, together, we've made so much progress.

Together, we're building a stronger economy and a better future for every Washingtonian.

Donate right now to help our campaign carry our strong economic message to every corner of Washington.

CONTRIBUTE: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTQ1OTg4NwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=MTAwMDE5NDEwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0

Thank you for standing with me. Trudi and I wish you and your family a relaxing, enjoyable, and safe Labor Day.

Very truly yours,

Jay Inslee

CONTRIBUTE: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=OTQ1OTg4NwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=MTAwMDE5NDEwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0

Contributions or gifts to Jay Inslee for Washington are not tax deductible. All content (c) Jay Inslee for Washington, All Rights Reserved 2016.

PO Box 21067
Seattle, WA 98111

name: =?utf-8?Q?Austin=20Chambers=2C=20EricGreitens.com?=
subject: Fwd: Mike Pence and Eric Greitens TOMORROW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Democrats Attack Greitens Days Before Missouri GOP Primary

By Maggie Severns, POLITICO
7/28/2016 5:17 PM EST

A political group affiliated with the Democratic Governors Association is reserving ad time to attack GOP gubernatorial candidate Eric Greitens in Missouri, seeking to affect the race just five days before Republicans choose their nominee from a crowded field.

Two sources tracking media spending in the state said that Jobs and Opportunity, a 527 group funded by the DGA, has booked about a half-million dollars in broadcast TV and additional radio spending attacking Greitens. The DGA did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The ad is set to start airing Friday. Audio, which a source said was from the ad, criticizes Greitens for accepting money from a donor accused of sexually abusing a woman.

"What does this victim accused of human trafficking have to do with your vote for governor? Well a man accused of abusing her for 13 years gave Eric Greitens $1 million," a narrator says in the ad. "While others returned his money, Greitens said he was honored to take it."

Greitens, a former Navy SEAL and political newcomer, has been picking up steam in the competitive GOP primary because of his charisma and status as an outsider. The ad is a clear indication that Democrats would rather not face Greitens in the general election. Polls have found the race between Greitens and the three other candidates extremely close, but a SurveyUSA poll released this week did find Greitens leading the pack with 25 percent, with opponent John Brunner behind him at 21 percent.

Missouri was the site of a notorious instance of Democratic primary meddling four years ago, when Democratic Sen. Claire McCaskill's campaign aired ads casting GOP Rep. Todd Akin as conservative before his Senate primary. Akin won the three-way Republican race and promptly imploded in the general election, losing to McCaskill by more than 15 percentage points.

The DGA tried to influence Republicans' gubernatorial primary in Colorado in 2014, funding a group that tried to boost conservative ex-Rep. Tom Tancredo. But the effort prompted a backlash and Tancredo ultimately lost the GOP primary.

The winner in the Missouri GOP primary will face current Attorney General Chris Koster, a moderate and rising star in the Democratic Party, in the general election.

Chip in now to help us fight back (https://donate.ericgreitens.com)

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

4579 Laclede Avenue #138
St. Louis, Missouri 63108

Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on Facebook (https://facebook.com/ericgreitens) and Twitter (https://twitter.com/ericgreitens) . Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected with the campaign. If they become too much, you can unsubscribe (http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=8f49c7802b) , but please let us know how we can improve. You can always reach us at team@ericgreitens.com (mailto:team@ericgreitens.com) or (314) 899-0288. You're still reading the fineprint? You must be a detail oriented, all-around awesome person who is a big supporter — you should volunteer (http://ericgreitens.com/volunteer) with us!
clean_body: Democrats Attack Greitens Days Before Missouri GOP Primary By Maggie Severns, POLITICO 7/28/2016 5:17 PM EST A political group affiliated with the Democratic Governors Association is reserving ad time to attack GOP gubernatorial candidate Eric Greitens in Missouri, seeking to affect the race just five days before Republicans choose their nominee from a crowded field. Two sources tracking media spending in the state said that Jobs and Opportunity, a 527 group funded by the DGA, has booked about a half-million dollars in broadcast TV and additional radio spending attacking Greitens. The DGA did not immediately respond to a request for comment. The ad is set to start airing Friday. Audio, which a source said was from the ad, criticizes Greitens for accepting money from a donor accused of sexually abusing a woman. "What does this victim accused of human trafficking have to do with your vote for governor? Well a man accused of abusing her for 13 years gave Eric Greitens $1 million," a narrator says in the ad. "While others returned his money, Greitens said he was honored to take it." Greitens, a former Navy SEAL and political newcomer, has been picking up steam in the competitive GOP primary because of his charisma and status as an outsider. The ad is a clear indication that Democrats would rather not face Greitens in the general election. Polls have found the race between Greitens and the three other candidates extremely close, but a SurveyUSA poll released this week did find Greitens leading the pack with 25 percent, with opponent John Brunner behind him at 21 percent. Missouri was the site of a notorious instance of Democratic primary meddling four years ago, when Democratic Sen. Claire McCaskill's campaign aired ads casting GOP Rep. Todd Akin as conservative before his Senate primary. Akin won the three-way Republican race and promptly imploded in the general election, losing to McCaskill by more than 15 percentage points. The DGA tried to influence Republicans' gubernatorial primary in Colorado in 2014, funding a group that tried to boost conservative ex-Rep. Tom Tancredo. But the effort prompted a backlash and Tancredo ultimately lost the GOP primary. The winner in the Missouri GOP primary will face current Attorney General Chris Koster, a moderate and rising star in the Democratic Party, in the general election. Chip in now to help us fight back () Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer 4579 Laclede Avenue #138 St. Louis, Missouri 63108 Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
